message:
docs(proposal/ft-y378j): decompose runtime_compat->runtime_async rename into 4 children

Reality-check finding (cc6 modes-of-reasoning review): AGENTS.md
states the rename is "pending under ft-g43fq" and the "compat" framing
retired, but the codebase tells a different story —
runtime_compat:: appears 695× in 80 src files + 494× in tests/
(~1,189 sites in frankenterm-core alone). Exactly 1 file uses the new
runtime_async:: name. The rename landed in name only; mass migration
has not happened.

Top offenders (refs in crates/frankenterm-core/src/):
  distributed.rs       65    storage.rs            32
  ipc.rs               50    snapshot_engine.rs    29
  vendored/mux_client  27    events.rs             26
  pool.rs              25    runtime.rs            23

A single sed sweep would land 1,189 sites in one commit — three
problems: (1) AGENTS.md no-deletion guard hits the same trip-wire
that blocked ars file deletes; (2) test churn vs production churn are
different review surfaces; (3) concurrent agent merge conflicts make
the monolithic commit fragile.

Apply the ft-j1qjt revert+decompose pattern (4994a1a9 →
ft-j1qjt.{1,3,2}) which proved 3-4 children with explicit ordering
ship in days vs stalling for weeks.

Filed children:
  ft-y378j.1  test-tree mechanical rename       (494 sites, low risk)
  ft-y378j.2  production source rename          (~620 sites, medium)
  ft-y378j.3  workspace audit + CI guard        (verify zero ref drift)
  ft-y378j.4  remove deprecation alias          (deferred 1 release)

Order is .1 → .2 → .3 → .4. Each step's success signal is the next
step's prerequisite.

Closes ft-y378j with the "superseded by .1-.4 children" note. Real
work continues there.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-8wt4o","title":"[gap] README runtime_compat quarantine claim not matched by frankenterm-core product paths","description":"README.md:41 claims only the remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses `runtime_compat`; grep shows live product modules still depend on `runtime_compat` in crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:37, crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:5918, crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:599, crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:1037, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:15,308.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:15.760863Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.893772Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:27.754618Z","close_reason":"Fixed in af925d26 — README runtime_compat boundary claim corrected to acknowledge multiple production seams","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
 {"id":"ft-8ybme","title":"[scan_pipeline] process_chunk allows append-after-flush-pending growth","description":"ChunkedPipelineState::process_chunk only exposes should_flush() as an advisory signal. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:221-223 and 450-516, callers can keep appending after the active raw-data buffer has already crossed max_buffer_bytes, so a stalled or forgotten flusher grows trigger_data_buffer/uncompressed_buffer without bound.

Repro:
1. let pipeline = ScanPipeline::default();
2. let mut state = ChunkedPipelineState::new(64);
3. pipeline.process_chunk(&vec![b'x'; 80], &mut state);
4. state.should_flush() == true
5. pipeline.process_chunk(&vec![b'y'; 80], &mut state);
6. raw replay buffer keeps growing instead of failing fast or forcing a flush.

Expected: once flush is pending, a subsequent non-empty process_chunk call should refuse to accumulate more bytes rather than silently converting backpressure into unbounded memory growth.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:55:00.532379Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:00:57.219221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:00:57.218185Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 658b945a: scan_pipeline now fails fast when flush is pending","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"ft-94juo","title":"[modes-of-reasoning] no CI guard against new sub-crate cycles — cargo cycles caught 3x during ft-y0loj extractions; needs pre-flight check","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:46:24.506909Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:26:27.804489Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:26:27.804218Z","close_reason":"Shipped at HEAD 72f08f98. scripts/check_workspace_cycles.sh + check_workspace_cycles.py implement a DFS cycle detector over cargo metadata --format-version=1 --no-deps. Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml as new step 'Workspace dep-graph cycle guard (ft-94juo)' immediately after ft-zoxxq.4 mux-interface guard. Verified: HEAD smoke-test detected 64 workspace members + 215 intra-workspace prod/build edges with ZERO cycles (exit 0). Synthetic 2-package mutual-dep JSON correctly detected with full edge trail (exit 1). Skips dev-dep edges to match cargo's own forgiveness. Surfaces FULL cycle with kind annotation rather than cargo's single-edge-pair error, plus a hint pointing at ft-usvnt resource-types extraction as the canonical resolution pattern. Mirror of ft-zoxxq.4 guard pattern (8d9fc273).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-9d4a3","title":"[MEDIUM] EventBus stats overreport queued backlog after subscribers drain channel","description":"Review finding in crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:655-674 and 1942-1970. EventBus::stats() trusts runtime_compat::broadcast_len() as unread queue depth, then derives oldest lag from that count. Under the shipped asupersync broadcast implementation, len() reports ring-buffer retention rather than unread messages; the existing test stats_clear_oldest_lag_when_queue_is_drained already documents this and had to weaken the assertion instead of expecting zero/None after the sole subscriber drains the channel. Repro: create EventBus::new(4), subscribe_deltas(), publish two SegmentCaptured events, recv both successfully, then call stats(); delta_queued can stay non-zero and delta_oldest_lag_ms can keep reporting stale lag even though no backlog remains. This causes operator/metrics surfaces (ipc status, metrics export) to falsely report event-bus congestion. Expected: stats() should report unread backlog/lag, or the fields/docs should be renamed to retained-ring occupancy rather than queue depth.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:14:13.498770Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:16:04.232903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:16:04.232646Z","close_reason":"Track unread backlog from slowest subscriber instead of retained ring occupancy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["patterns","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-9unrg","title":"frankenterm-core no-default-features fails: ars_drift tests miss DriftMonitor import","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:09:49.756811Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:46.370803Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:46.370517Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-9xbxe","title":"mcp_tools test helper hits asupersync vs fastmcp_rust::asupersync version conflict","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4812\` cannot compile under
\`--features mcp\`:

\`\`\`
error[E0308]: mismatched types
    --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4812:25
     |
4812 |         McpContext::new(asupersync::Cx::for_testing(), 1)
     |         --------------- ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
     |                         expected \`fastmcp_rust::Cx\`,
     |                         found \`asupersync::Cx\`
     |
note: there are multiple different versions of crate \`asupersync\` in
      the dependency graph
\`\`\`

\`McpContext::new\` (defined in \`fastmcp_rust\`) expects
\`fastmcp_rust::Cx\` (i.e. fastmcp_rust's own vendored copy of
asupersync's Cx type), but the test helper passes the directly-imported
\`asupersync::Cx::for_testing()\` from the workspace's pinned
asupersync crate. With version drift between fastmcp_rust's vendored
asupersync (0.2.6 per the older fastmcp_rust pin) and the workspace
asupersync (0.2.9 per /Users/jemanuel/.cargo/registry path in the
error message), the two Cx types are nominally distinct even though
their fields are identical.

## Concrete failure path

1. Run \`cargo test --no-run --lib -p frankenterm-core --features mcp\`.
2. Compiler tries to build the \`#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]\` test module
   in mcp_tools.rs.
3. \`test_mcp_context()\` at line 4811-4813 fails to type-check because
   \`McpContext::new\` from fastmcp_rust expects \`fastmcp_rust::Cx\` but
   the call site passes \`asupersync::Cx\` from the workspace's
   asupersync.
4. **Entire mcp_tools test compilation blocked** — none of the ~50+
   tests in the mcp_tools test module can run, including any
   regression tests filed against mcp_tools handlers.

## Reproduction

\`\`\`
CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \\
CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \\
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p0-target \\
cargo test --no-run --lib -p frankenterm-core \\
  --no-default-features --features mcp 2>&1 | grep -B2 -A10 E0308
\`\`\`

## Severity rationale

- Blocks mcp_tools test verification entirely — any new mcp_tools test
  added under \`--features mcp\` cannot be verified locally.
- Real defect-finding work in the mcp surface (e.g. ft-tzwuw which
  found 3 missing timeout_secs guards) lands without test coverage
  because the test target won't compile.
- Not a runtime bug in production code — fastmcp_rust vs asupersync
  Cx structs are runtime-compatible; only the type system rejects
  them.

## Fix sketch

One of:
1. Bump the fastmcp_rust pin so its vendored asupersync matches the
   workspace asupersync 0.2.9 (preferred — single source of truth).
2. Replace the \`test_mcp_context\` helper to use
   \`fastmcp_rust::Cx::for_testing()\` (whatever the equivalent is in
   the fastmcp_rust API surface) instead of bridging through
   asupersync directly.
3. Add an explicit conversion (\`From<asupersync::Cx> for
   fastmcp_rust::Cx\`) in fastmcp_rust if the types are
   field-compatible.

(1) is the cleanest because it eliminates the duplicated dependency.
(2) is the smallest local change.

## Scope

Diagnostic only — fix likely lives in fastmcp_rust pin (Cargo.toml)
or the test helper. Not in scope for the agent that filed this bead
unless they have context on which fastmcp_rust SHA contains the
asupersync 0.2.9 alignment.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:52:59.938463Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.989722Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.989533Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-xxp05","title":"recorder health_with_cx preserves backend kind on cancellation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T04:21:05.494076Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:32:17.843593Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:32:17.843149Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-xydxz","title":"[review][LOW] ft-eze48 ftvi fuzz corpus has only one seed (FTVI magic + EOF), sparse coverage for libfuzzer starting points","description":"commit 6567397b landed fuzz/fuzz_targets/ftvi_index_from_bytes.rs with a single corpus seed at fuzz/corpus/ftvi_index_from_bytes/short_magic. Hex dump confirms it is 5 bytes: 46 54 56 49 0a (the real magic b'FTVI' followed by 0x0a).

The seed uses the correct magic bytes (verified against crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/vector_index.rs:10 const MAGIC = b'FTVI'). That is the good part — it seeds libfuzzer at the real format boundary, not random garbage.

Gap: only ONE seed, covering only ONE failure shape (magic-matches-but-truncated-at-version). A properly-seeded fuzz target typically ships 5-10 seeds exercising:
- Empty input (zero bytes)
- Minimal valid FTVI: magic + version=1 + dimension=0 + count=0 (12 bytes total, no records)
- Single-record FTVI: magic + version=1 + dimension=1 + count=1 + one (u64 id, u16 f16-value) record
- Magic-only (4 bytes — truncates before version)
- Valid magic + valid version + truncated at dimension (6 bytes)
- Valid header claiming count=1 + zero record bytes (parser must not OOB)
- Valid header + bogus version (e.g., version=255) to exercise version rejection

The Structured/Arbitrary lane at :66-84 generates real FTVI buffers at runtime via write_ftvi_vec, so coverage CAN be reached, but libfuzzer's coverage-guided exploration benefits from diverse starting seeds — especially for the header state machine, where coverage-guided mutation is more productive when seeded at valid-header boundaries.

Fix: add 5-7 curated seed files to fuzz/corpus/ftvi_index_from_bytes/ constructed via write_ftvi_vec and dd/printf. One-time scripting cost.

Severity: LOW — harness works as-is; this is a corpus-quality improvement, not a correctness gap. Audit round 14, 2026-04-23.

Tag: review, fuzz, ftvi, corpus-quality.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:37:51.674698Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:52:07.939748Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:52:07.939424Z","close_reason":"Already shipped in 87afb28e: add four curated ftvi corpus seeds","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-y0loj","title":"[STRATEGIC/MEDIUM] frankenterm-core is a 488-file / ~854K-line monolith — every consumer pays full compile cost; feature-flag N×M matrix uncitable","description":"Modes-of-reasoning F5: lib.rs has 403 pub mod/mod in 538 lines. No sub-crates for MCP/connectors/ARS/patterns/mission/tx/fleet/native-mux. +{"id":"ft-y378j.1","title":"[ft-y378j.1] mechanical sed rename runtime_compat -> runtime_async in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/","description":"Step 1 of the ft-y378j decomposition (see docs/proposals/ft-y378j-rename-decomposition.md). Sed-driven mechanical rename of all 494 runtime_compat:: refs across crates/frankenterm-core/tests/. Tests use the runtime primitives mostly for setup (block_on, channel constructors) — low risk surface, ideal first step. +{"id":"ft-y378j.2","title":"[ft-y378j.2] production source rename runtime_compat -> runtime_async in non-runtime modules","description":"Step 2 of ft-y378j decomposition. The ~620 production refs outside runtime.rs / runtime_compat.rs / cancellation.rs itself. Top offenders: distributed (65), ipc (50), storage (32), snapshot_engine (29), vendored/mux_client (27), events (26), pool (25). Bead ft-y378j.1 must land first so test rename is validated independently. Verify: cargo check -p frankenterm-core clean after rename; only the runtime_compat.rs file itself + the deprecated re-export alias still mention the old name.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:27:49.843026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:27:49.843026Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["refactor","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y378j.2","depends_on_id":"ft-y378j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:27:49.843026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-y378j.3","title":"[ft-y378j.3] workspace-wide rename audit + CI guard preventing re-introduction","description":"Step 3 of ft-y378j decomposition. After .1 and .2 land: (a) sweep crates/frankenterm-core-* sub-crates and frankenterm/* vendored crates for any remaining runtime_compat:: refs and migrate them; (b) add scripts/check_runtime_async_imports.sh to fail CI if a new file outside the deprecated alias module introduces 'runtime_compat::' (mirrors ft-zoxxq.4 / ft-8smkj guard pattern). Acceptance: rg runtime_compat:: workspace-wide returns hits ONLY in the runtime_compat.rs file (the alias) + the deprecation re-export comment block.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:27:50.092225Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:27:50.092225Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","refactor","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y378j.3","depends_on_id":"ft-y378j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:27:50.092225Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-y378j.4","title":"[ft-y378j.4] remove pub use runtime_async as runtime_compat deprecation alias after one release cycle","description":"Step 4 (deferred) of ft-y378j decomposition. Schedule for after one release has shipped post-.3 landing. Removes the 'pub use runtime_async as runtime_compat;' alias and deletes the runtime_compat.rs shim entirely. AGENTS.md note that the rename was pending becomes a historical artifact; CI guard from .3 stays in place to prevent future re-introduction. Verify: workspace-wide rg returns 0 hits for runtime_compat (the symbol) anywhere except the proposal docs / commit history references.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:27:50.344051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:27:50.344051Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deferred","refactor","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y378j.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y378j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:27:50.344051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-y87lf","title":"pdu_stream_decode ASan allocation-size-too-big on new fuzz artifact","description":"`cargo +nightly fuzz run pdu_stream_decode --features codec-fuzz-targets -- -max_total_time=120 -detect_leaks=0` found a new crash in `codec::Pdu::stream_decode` under ASan.

Artifact: `fuzz/artifacts/pdu_stream_decode/crash-22974e1f96d936743995342b9a12dc33a8af8f51`
Repro: `cargo fuzz run --features=codec-fuzz-targets pdu_stream_decode fuzz/artifacts/pdu_stream_decode/crash-22974e1f96d936743995342b9a12dc33a8af8f51`

Observed failure: `AddressSanitizer: requested allocation size 0x1fffffd2000000 exceeds maximum supported size` followed by `SIGABRT`. This looks like a new oversized-allocation path despite the existing OOM regression coverage in `frankenterm/codec/tests/regression_oom_crasher.rs`.

The short soak also grew corpus coverage substantially before the abort; no other new artifacts were emitted during the run.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:36.123052Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:44:35.881131Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:44:35.880846Z","close_reason":"Bounded ClusteredLine and codec decode allocations; pinned the 22974e fuzz crasher in regression and corpus","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb","title":"[EPIC] Transaction execution data-plane: connect orchestration to real pane operations, policy gates, and agent dispatch","description":"# Goal
Bridge the gap between FrankenTerm's well-engineered tx/mission orchestration framework and actual execution against live panes. Today the framework provides type-safe state machines, idempotency ledgers, observability hooks, and resume semantics — but step execution against panes is synthetic. This epic wires the data plane so that ft tx run actually does things.

# Background (reality check 2026-04-06, updated after three verification passes)
- tx_execution.rs (1,663 lines, 28 tests): StepExecutor trait with 3 methods. SyntheticStepExecutor for testing, PolicyPrepareStepExecutor for real gate evaluation (DONE). TxExecutionEngine<E: StepExecutor> orchestrates full lifecycle but is NOT used by the CLI.
- plan.rs (7,760 lines, 123 tests): Core types solid. Gate evaluation real via TxPreparePolicyAuthorizer/TxPrepareApprovalChecker/TxPrepareTargetLookup traits with StorageBacked impls. StepAction enum (line 529) has SendText, WaitFor, AcquireLock, etc.
- tx_idempotency.rs (2,041 lines, 68 tests): Strong implementation. Integrated in TxExecutionEngine.
- mission_loop.rs (5,291 lines, 116 tests): Real evaluation loop but dispatch is not connected.
- tx_observability.rs (1,287 lines, 20 tests): Event taxonomy complete, integrated in TxExecutionEngine.

# Critical CLI Finding (pass 3)
Both ft tx run AND ft robot tx run import:
  tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all as build_robot_tx_prepare_gate_inputs
This means the CLI BYPASSES all policy gates despite SnowyHorse's PolicyPrepareStepExecutor work. The CLI also doesn't use TxExecutionEngine — it manually calls evaluate_prepare_phase / execute_commit_phase with synthetic inputs. The ft-y9lnb.6 bead addresses this.

# What's Done
- Policy gate wiring (ft-y9lnb.2, closed): prepare-phase traits implemented with StorageBacked adapters.

# What Remains
1. PaneStepExecutor: real execute_steps() and execute_compensations() (ft-y9lnb.1)
2. Mission dispatch: MissionDispatchContract -> WorkflowRunner bridge (ft-y9lnb.3)
3. Timeout/backpressure: per-step/phase/tx timeouts + FleetPressureTier (ft-y9lnb.4)
4. Integration tests: 18 scenarios via TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor> (ft-y9lnb.5)
5. CLI wiring: ft tx run/rollback use TxExecutionEngine + PaneStepExecutor, add --dry-run (ft-y9lnb.6)

# Acceptance Criteria
1. ft tx run executes real pane operations via PaneStepExecutor, not synthetic stubs.
2. Both human CLI and Robot Mode use real policy gates (no more allow_all import).
3. ft tx rollback sends real compensation commands.
4. ft tx run --dry-run validates permissions without executing (new user safety feature).
5. TxExecutionEngine orchestrates the lifecycle (not manual phase calls in main.rs).
6. Every step produces TxObservabilityEvent audit trail + idempotency ledger entries.
7. 18+ integration tests + 3 E2E scenarios prove the full pipeline.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:12.486800Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:58:22.449058Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:58:22.448822Z","close_reason":"All 6 child beads closed: .1 PaneStepExecutor, .2 policy gates, .3 mission dispatch, .4 timeouts/backpressure, .5 integration tests (30 tests), .6 CLI wiring. The tx data-plane is fully wired: ft tx run/rollback uses real PaneStepExecutor with policy gates, timeouts, backpressure, and falls back to SyntheticStepExecutor when backend unavailable.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data-plane","goal-line","mission","tx"]}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.1","title":"Implement PaneStepExecutor: connect tx step execution to Robot Mode send/wait-for primitives","description":"# Goal
Create a PaneStepExecutor that implements the StepExecutor trait by dispatching step operations to actual panes via WeztermInterface.

# Critical Design Constraint: Sync Trait Inside Async Runtime
The StepExecutor trait is SYNC (returns Vec<T>). But the entire CLI runs inside rt.block_on(async { ... }) (main.rs line 16248). WeztermInterface methods are async (return Pin<Box<dyn Future>>). Calling block_on() inside an existing block_on() panics (nested runtime).

## Solution: Approach A (RECOMMENDED) — Make TxExecutionEngine async-capable
Add a parallel async trait:

    pub trait AsyncStepExecutor {
        async fn evaluate_gates(&self, contract: &MissionTxContract, now_ms: i64) -> Vec<TxPrepareGateInput>;
        async fn execute_steps(&self, contract: &MissionTxContract, fail_step: Option<&str>, now_ms: i64) -> Vec<TxCommitStepInput>;
        async fn execute_compensations(&self, commit_report: &TxCommitReport, fail_for_step: Option<&str>, now_ms: i64) -> Vec<TxCompensationStepInput>;
    }

Then either:
- Add TxExecutionEngine::execute_async() that takes AsyncStepExecutor, OR
- Make TxExecutionEngine generic over both sync and async executors via a unified trait with async fn

PaneStepExecutor implements AsyncStepExecutor. It can .await WeztermInterface calls directly since the CLI is already in an async context.

SyntheticStepExecutor and PolicyPrepareStepExecutor keep the sync trait. A blanket impl wraps sync executors into async ones.

## Alternative: Approach B — Use tokio::task::spawn_blocking
Call the sync StepExecutor methods from a blocking thread that CAN create a new runtime:
    let result = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
        let rt = RuntimeBuilder::current_thread().build().unwrap();
        rt.block_on(async { handle.send_text(pane_id, &text).await })
    }).await;

This works but adds thread-hop overhead per step. Acceptable for the MVP.

## RECOMMENDATION
Start with Approach B (spawn_blocking) for the MVP — it's simpler and doesn't require changing TxExecutionEngine. Then migrate to Approach A in a follow-up bead if performance matters.

# Verified Types (plan.rs)
- StepAction enum (line 529): SendText { pane_id, text, paste_mode }, WaitFor { pane_id, condition, timeout_ms }, AcquireLock, ReleaseLock, StoreData, RunWorkflow, MarkEventHandled, ValidateApproval, NestedPlan, Custom
- TxStep { step_id: TxStepId, ordinal: usize, action: StepAction, description: String }
- TxCommitStepInput { step_id, success: bool, reason_code, error_code: Option, completed_at_ms }
- TxCompensation { for_step_id, action: StepAction }
- TxCompensationStepInput { for_step_id, success, reason_code, error_code: Option, completed_at_ms }

# WeztermInterface methods (verified)
- send_text(pane_id: u64, text: &str) -> WeztermFuture<()>
- send_text_with_options(pane_id, text, no_paste, no_newline) -> WeztermFuture<()>
- get_text(pane_id: u64, escapes: bool) -> WeztermFuture<String>
- list_panes() -> WeztermFuture<Vec<PaneInfo>>

# Design: PaneStepExecutor
## evaluate_gates(): delegate to PolicyPrepareStepExecutor (sync, no I/O, no nesting issue)

## execute_steps(): iterate contract.plan.steps, for each TxStep match on action:
- SendText { pane_id, text, paste_mode } -> spawn_blocking + block_on(handle.send_text(...))
- WaitFor { pane_id, condition, timeout_ms } -> spawn_blocking + block_on(poll get_text until match or timeout)
- StoreData { key, value } -> StorageHandle (sync, no nesting issue)
- AcquireLock/ReleaseLock -> PaneWorkflowLockManager if available
- Unsupported (RunWorkflow, NestedPlan, Custom, etc.) -> success: false, reason_code: "unsupported_action"
- On fail_step match: inject deterministic failure

## execute_compensations(): same dispatch but iterating contract.plan.compensations

# Unit Tests (12 minimum)
1. execute_steps SendText happy path: 3 steps, MockWezterm send_text succeed -> 3 success inputs
2. execute_steps SendText pane not found: send_text error -> failed input
3. execute_steps WaitFor match: get_text returns matching text -> success
4. execute_steps WaitFor timeout: pattern not found in time -> failed input
5. execute_steps StoreData: key/value stored -> success
6. execute_steps unsupported action: RunWorkflow -> failed with unsupported_action
7. execute_steps fail_step injection: forced failure at named step
8. execute_steps mixed: 3 steps, step 2 fails -> step 3 not executed
9. execute_compensations happy: compensation text sent -> success
10. execute_compensations failure: compensation send fails -> failed
11. execute_compensations empty: no compensations -> empty vec
12. evaluate_gates delegates: same results as PolicyPrepareStepExecutor

NOTE ON TESTS: Unit tests use MockWezterm which is NOT async — mock_wezterm_handle() returns synchronous mocks. Tests can use the simpler approach. The spawn_blocking path is only needed in the production code path where real WeztermInterface is genuinely async.

# Structured Logging
- tracing::info! per step: step_id, action variant, pane_id, duration_ms
- tracing::warn! on failure with error context
- tracing::instrument on execute_steps and execute_compensations

# Acceptance Criteria
1. PaneStepExecutor implements all 3 StepExecutor trait methods.
2. evaluate_gates delegates to PolicyPrepareStepExecutor.
3. execute_steps dispatches real WeztermInterface calls for SendText, WaitFor, StoreData.
4. Works when called from inside rt.block_on(async { ... }) (the CLI context).
5. All 12+ unit tests pass.
6. No unsafe code.
7. File: crates/frankenterm-core/src/tx_execution.rs (alongside existing executors).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:33.101908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:50:14.230331Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:50:14.230100Z","close_reason":"Implemented PaneStepExecutor with 13 passing tests. Dispatches SendText, WaitFor, StoreData, AcquireLock, ReleaseLock, MarkEventHandled, ValidateApproval to real WeztermInterface. Failure boundary stops execution after first failed step. Uses thread::spawn + fresh runtime for sync->async bridge.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["executor","robot-mode","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:33.101908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@
+# Proposal: decompose the `runtime_compat → runtime_async` rename
+
+**Bead:** [ft-y378j](../../.beads/issues.jsonl) — closing as superseded by .1-.4 children
+**Related:** ft-7iof6 (the original "runtime_compat is canonical, not compat" reframe), AGENTS.md §"Async Runtime: asupersync"
+
+## Reality check
+
+AGENTS.md states that the rename to `runtime_async` is "pending under
+ft-g43fq" and that `runtime_compat` is the "canonical async API
+surface" with the "compat" framing retired. The cc6 modes-of-reasoning
+review at HEAD found this framing optimistic:
+
+```
+$ rg -l 'runtime_compat::' crates/frankenterm-core/src/      | wc -l
+80
+$ rg 'runtime_compat::' crates/frankenterm-core/src/         | wc -l
+695
+$ rg 'runtime_async::'   crates/frankenterm-core/src/        | wc -l
+1
+```
+
+695 inline references across 80 source files plus another **494 in
+the tests/ tree** (total ~1,189 sites in `frankenterm-core` alone).
+Exactly 1 file uses the new name. The "rename" landed in name only —
+mass migration has not happened.
+
+Top offenders by reference count:
+
+| file                                       | refs |
+|--------------------------------------------|-----:|
+| `distributed.rs`                           | 65   |
+| `ipc.rs`                                   | 50   |
+| `storage.rs`                               | 32   |
+| `snapshot_engine.rs`                       | 29   |
+| `vendored/mux_client.rs`                   | 27   |
+| `events.rs`                                | 26   |
+| `pool.rs`                                  | 25   |
+| `runtime.rs`                               | 23   |
+| `wezterm.rs`                               | 22   |
+| `tx_execution.rs`                          | 21   |
+
+## Why decompose
+
+A single mass `sed s/runtime_compat/runtime_async/g` across the
+workspace would touch ~1,189 sites in one commit. Three problems:
+
+1. **AGENTS.md no-deletion guard.** Renaming a re-export module path
+   inside `frankenterm-core/src/` triggers the same hook that blocked
+   the ars-extraction file deletes (ref ft-mr35k commit 876537fc).
+   The mass-rename commit would need either a deliberate hook bypass
+   or a careful split.
+2. **Test churn vs production churn.** Tests use the runtime
+   primitives mostly for setup (`runtime_compat::block_on`, channel
+   constructors); production uses them for hot-path orchestration.
+   These are different review surfaces and reverting is easier when
+   they're separate commits.
+3. **Concurrent agent coordination.** With ~10 sub-crates extracted
+   under ft-y0loj.* and live cc6/cc7 lanes, a 1,189-site touch in one
+   commit makes merge conflicts inevitable. Smaller commits land
+   atomically against whatever lands in parallel.
+
+The ft-j1qjt revert+decompose pattern (4994a1a9 → ft-j1qjt.{1,3,2})
+proved that splitting a too-big-to-land bead into 3-4 children with
+explicit ordering ships in days instead of stalling for weeks. Apply
+the same template here.
+
+## Decomposition
+
+| bead          | scope                                                                | risk  |
+|---------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|-------|
+| ft-y378j.1    | mechanical sed-driven rename of `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/**` (494 sites) — test setup only, no production code path changes | low   |
+| ft-y378j.2    | production source rename in non-runtime modules (the ~620 sites outside `runtime.rs` / `runtime_compat.rs` / `cancellation.rs` itself) | medium |
+| ft-y378j.3    | workspace-wide audit + verify: `rg runtime_compat::` returns zero hits in non-deprecated paths; the deprecated alias still re-exports for one release; CI guard added to prevent re-introduction | medium |
+| ft-y378j.4    | remove the `pub use runtime_async as runtime_compat;` deprecation alias after one release cycle has passed since .3 landed | low   |
+
+Order: **.1 → .2 → .3 → .4**. Each step's success signal is the next
+step's prerequisite — `.2` should not start until `.1` lands because
+the test surface changes in `.1` would otherwise have to be
+re-resolved against `.2`'s production rename.
+
+## Why not a single sed sweep with git hook bypass
+
+Tempting (it's a syntactic rename, after all), but two things make
+the four-step path safer:
+
+- **Linter agent re-formats per file.** When a single 1,189-site
+  commit gets reformatted by the linter agent, you can't tell which
+  hunks are intentional and which are linter noise. Smaller commits
+  let reviewers diff each step against a clean baseline.
+- **Failure isolation.** If ft-y378j.2 introduces a compile error
+  somewhere unexpected, reverting `.2` doesn't lose the test-side
+  rename `.1` already validated. A monolithic commit forces an
+  all-or-nothing revert.
+
+## Acceptance for THIS proposal
+
+- [x] Reality numbers verified: 695 src refs / 494 test refs / 1 new-name ref.
+- [ ] Children ft-y378j.{1,2,3,4} filed under ft-y378j.
+- [ ] ft-y378j closed with "superseded" note pointing at the children.
+
+## Cross-references
+
+- AGENTS.md §"Async Runtime: asupersync" — the framing this proposal
+  reality-checks.
+- `docs/proposals/ft-7iof6-runtime-compat-canonical-surface.md` — the
+  original ft-7iof6 reframe; .1-.4 implement what it described.
+- ft-j1qjt revert at baef663e — the decomposition pattern this
+  proposal copies.
